name: BLIFFERT RES (KYLE)
address: 1814 NW WILD RYE CIRCLE, BEND, OR 97703

fr.recommendations: Ensuring that the single access point is clearly marked and unobstructed by any dense vegetation could significantly enhance emergency responder access and movement within the property. Consideration for a secondary access point, if feasible, may provide alternative routes for emergency personnel in critical situations. Regular maintenance of the landscaped area to control dense vegetation could facilitate better visibility and navigation for responders, supporting effective operational efforts. Keeping pedestrian walkways unobstructed and well-defined would support efficient movement throughout the property, allowing emergency crews to quickly access vital parts of the premises. In addition, ensuring that open green spaces remain free from clutter could support responder staging and equipment placement, optimizing the overall emergency response effectiveness.

wildfire.recommendations: Maintaining the open terrain and existing defensible space around the property may improve wildfire resilience by reducing opportunities for fire to spread. Enhancing the separation between vegetation and the structure, particularly where vegetation is adjacent to the building, could further support structure exposure reduction. Addressing the areas with scattered shrubs by reducing their density may decrease fuel continuity and offer added protection in case of fire. Preserving the open fuel break around the perimeter will maintain an essential buffer, while managing the cluster of trees to ensure spacing between them could reduce the potential for crown fire spread. Consistent vegetation management, particularly along the vegetated perimeter, would aid in minimizing fuel loads and support long-term property preparedness and suppression efforts.
